rationale_for_baseline_tier: Decompensated right heart failure (cor pulmonale) in severe COPD with resting hypoxaemia (~89% on room air), visible cyanosis and accessory-muscle use. The in-person clinician could manage this at home only because they examined the patient, measured vitals, gave an immediate diuretic dose and titrated oxygen; a telehealth service has none of those levers and cannot exclude acute deterioration or a subacute PE. The honest floor is therefore a same-day in-person/ED assessment (T4) for examination, controlled oxygen titration, cautious diuresis with monitoring, ECG and echo. It is not (yet) an immediate 000 ambulance because the patient is currently alert and speaking in full sentences — but the 000 triggers must be safety-netted.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening confusion, extreme drowsiness, breathlessness despite oxygen, or inability to speak in full sentences
escalation_edges.rationale: These indicate CO2 narcosis / decompensated respiratory failure or cardiac collapse — a life-threatening emergency needing an ambulance within minutes.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes confused or very drowsy, can't speak in full sentences, or his breathing gets worse even with the oxygen on — call 000 for an ambulance straight away and tell them he has severe lung disease and heart strain.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New chest pain, sudden severe breathlessness, collapse or fainting
escalation_edges.rationale: Raises an acute cardiac event or pulmonary embolism precipitating collapse — immediate ambulance and emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he gets chest pain, suddenly can't breathe, or collapses or faints — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline presentation — worsening breathlessness with new bilateral swelling and abdominal bloating, hypoxaemia and cyanosis, but currently alert and speaking in sentences
escalation_edges.rationale: Right heart decompensation with resting hypoxaemia that cannot be examined, dosed or monitored over telehealth requires same-day in-person assessment, not home management.
escalation_edges.communication_template: You need to be seen in person today — please go to (or be taken to) an emergency department or urgent same-day clinic so a doctor can examine you, check your heart tracing and bloods, sort out your oxygen and start the right fluid treatment.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Oxygen must be given at the prescribed controlled low flow only; do not advise the patient to uptitrate oxygen to correct the SpO2, as this risks hypercapnic respiratory failure. Escalation should ensure blood-gas assessment before any oxygen change.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Housebound, mobility limited to the home, reliant on daughter who is present for the call
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: The daughter can arrange and accompany urgent transport and watch for the deterioration triggers; confirm she can get him to same-day assessment or call 000 if he worsens. Decreased mobility also raises pressure-injury and falls risk.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need a same-day in-person assessment today — an emergency department or an urgent same-day clinic — because the heart and lung strain you're describing has to be examined, and needs a heart tracing, blood tests and a heart ultrasound that can't be done over a video call. In the meantime, use your prescribed home oxygen continuously at the flow it's set to (do not turn it up), keep your legs raised when resting, and have your daughter stay with you. Call 000 immediately if you become confused or very drowsy, can't speak in full sentences, your breathing gets worse despite the oxygen, or you get chest pain or collapse.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person assessment (ED or urgent care) today; call 000 for an ambulance if any red-flag deterioration occurs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours, still seek same-day assessment via the emergency department; 000 is available 24 hours a day for the red-flag triggers.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Bring the reason for referral (new decompensated right heart failure / cor pulmonale on severe COPD with resting hypoxaemia). GP review within 48 hours and an urgent outpatient echocardiogram were arranged; electrolytes/renal function need checking before and during any diuresis.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as home-manageable or a routine/prompt GP problem despite resting hypoxaemia, cyanosis and new decompensated right heart failure.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Delayed recognition allows progression to hypercapnic respiratory failure and cardiac collapse at home.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Same-day in-person review is acceptable as a minimum, but a lower-acuity same-day clinic without ready access to bloods/ECG/oxygen titration may under-serve the hypoxaemia.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance for a currently-alert patient speaking in sentences is defensible over-triage given the hypoxaemia and cyanosis, and is far preferable to under-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The confirmatory examination (JVP, RV heave, loud P2, S3, ascites) and investigations (ECG, bloods, echo) that separate cor pulmonale from a COPD flare, biventricular failure, subacute PE or primary liver disease cannot be performed over telehealth, and the home SpO2 is confounded by a chronically low COPD baseline.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the most likely syndrome (right heart decompensation), acknowledge the mimics that cannot be excluded remotely, and escalate to same-day in-person/ED assessment rather than committing to home management.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Assuming it is 'just a COPD flare', starting or dosing a diuretic remotely, advising the patient to increase their oxygen, or reassuring and deferring to a routine appointment.
